title: nf-core/pacvar pipeline parameters
description: A variant caller for PacBio long read sequencing - also can be used for PureTarget panel analysis, and quantification of repeat expansions

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/pacvar/usage#samplesheet-input).

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

skip_ensemblvep.description: Option to skip SNVs annotation with Ensembl VEP.
skip_ensemblvep.help_text:
Set to 'false' will enable SNVs annotation with Ensembl VEP. 
Note: VEP annotation is currently unsupported for 'CHM13v2.0' as a compatible VEP cache is not provided in the standard configuration. See https://annotation-cache.github.io/vep_cache/ for availiable vep cache.

vep_custom_args_snv.description: Add an extra custom argument to VEP.
vep_custom_args_snv.help_text: Using this params you can add custom args to VEP. Avoid using `--merged` and `--refseq` because https://annotation-cache.github.io/vep_cache/ does not contain `homo_sapien_refseq` and `homo_sapiens_merged` folder.

vep_custom_args_sv.description: Add extra custom arguments to VEP for SV annotation.
vep_custom_args_sv.help_text: Using this parameter you can add SV-specific custom arguments to VEP. Avoid using `--merged` and `--refseq` because https://annotation-cache.github.io/vep_cache/ does not contain `homo_sapien_refseq` and `homo_sapiens_merged` folder.

vep_custom_args_cnv.description: Add extra custom arguments to VEP for CNV annotation.
vep_custom_args_cnv.help_text: Using this parameter you can add CNV-specific custom arguments to VEP. Avoid using `--merged` and `--refseq` because https://annotation-cache.github.io/vep_cache/ does not contain `homo_sapien_refseq` and `homo_sapiens_merged` folder.

outdir_vep_cache.description: Output directory where the downloaded VEP cache will be saved. Only used when `--download_vep_cache true`. Use absolute paths on cloud infrastructure. Note: when set, the cache is published outside `--outdir`.

vep_out_format.help_text: Sets the format of the output-file from VEP. Available formats: json, tab and vcf.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

genome.help_text:
If using a reference genome configured in the pipeline using iGenomes, use this parameter to give the ID for the reference. This is then used to build the full paths for all required reference genome files e.g. `--genome GRCh38`. 

See the [nf-core website docs](https://nf-co.re/usage/reference_genomes) for more details.

fasta.description: Path to FASTA genome or MMI file.
fasta.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--genome` is not specified. If you don't have a BWA index available this will be generated for you automatically. Combine with `--save_reference` to save BWA index for future runs.

igenomes_ignore.description: Do not load the iGenomes reference config.
igenomes_ignore.help_text: Do not load `igenomes.config` when running the pipeline. You may choose this option if you observe clashes between custom parameters and those supplied in `igenomes.config`.

igenomes_base.description: The base path to the igenomes reference files

dbsnp_tbi.description: Path to index file of the dbsnp VCF file

dict.description: Path to dict for variant calling

download_vep_cache.help_text: Set this parameter will download vep cache even if --vep_cache are provided.

vep_cache.help_text:
Path to VEP cache which should contain the relevant species, genome and build directories at the path ${vep_species}/${vep_genome}_${vep_cache_version}

If you use AWS iGenomes, this has already been set for you appropriately.

vep_cache_version.help_text:
Alternative cache version can be used to specify the correct Ensembl Genomes version number as these differ from the concurrent Ensembl/VEP version numbers.

If you use AWS iGenomes, this has already been set for you appropriately.

vep_genome.help_text:
This is used to specify the genome when looking for local cache, or cloud based cache.

If you use AWS iGenomes, this has already been set for you appropriately.

vep_species.help_text:
Alternatively species listed in Ensembl Genomes caches can be used.

If you use AWS iGenomes, this has already been set for you appropriately.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

variant.title: Variant Calling options
variant.help_text: These options are specific to pacvar and allow customization of alignment and phasing of the variants

snv_caller.description: The tool to use for calling SNP variants

sv_caller.description: The tool to use for calling structural variants

skip_snp.description: Option to skip SNP variant calling

skip_sv.description: Option to skip SV variant calling

skip_hificnv.description: Skip HiFiCNV copy number variant calling

expected_cn.description: BED/BED.GZ file specifying expected copy-number regions (for sawfish/hificnv).

cnv_excluded_regions.description: BED/BED.GZ file of regions to exclude from CNV calling.

methylation_profiling.title: Methylation profiling options
methylation_profiling.description: Options for summarizing CpG site probabilities for 5mC methylation.
methylation_profiling.help_text: These options control 5mC methylation probability calling from PacBio long-read sequencing data.

skip_cpg.description: Skip CpG site probability for 5mC methylation calling
skip_cpg.help_text: When enabled, the WGS workflow will skip CpG site 5mC methylation probability scoring.

skip_fiberseq.description: Skip Fiber-seq nucleosome annotation with fibertools-rs
skip_fiberseq.help_text: When enabled, the WGS workflow will skip adding Fiber-seq BAM auxiliary tags for nucleosome and MSP positions with fibertools-rs.

repeat.title: Options specific to the repeat workflow
repeat.help_text: These options are important for the repeat-characterization step of the workflow

repeat_id.description: If no sample-specific repeat ID to be plotted is provided in the samplesheet, this value will be used

pre_processing.title: Pre-processing options that are not workflow specific
pre_processing.help_text: These options are important for the pre-processign step of the workflow which includes skip_demultiplexing

intervals.description: Path to BED file containing intervals for either variant calling or for repeat expansion characterization

general_workflow_options.title: General workflow options
general_workflow_options.help_text: These options are important for the specifying which workflow runs

workflow.description: Option to choose which workflow type to run

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
